hypothesis: If crystal structures are perturbed by realistic lattice (σl) and coordinate (σc) noise, then gradient-descent optimization of XRD-similarity losses (negative cosine similarity, MSE, negative entropy) will fail to reliably recover the ground-truth structures (low Match Rate), even when enforcing space-group symmetry via projected gradient descent, because the XRD loss landscape is highly nonconvex and populated with many local minima that trap gradient-based optimizers.
